mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held below 0.25 percent signals an accommodative monetary stance that lowers the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically raising present equity values and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following months with FEDFUNDS below 0.25% are not significantly positive compared to the unconditional mean (two-sided t-test p > 0.05), or the strategy Sharpe ratio over a 3-year rolling window falls below 0.3.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 7.0 percent triggers a flight-to-safety response as recession probabilities surge, causing institutional investors to reduce equity beta and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations where UNRATE is above 7.0 percent, or the average return in triggered episodes is not negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned near zero while consumer prices are elevated, deeply negative real policy rates force capital out of money-market instruments and into risk assets, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with elevated inflation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not positive in at least 60% of joint trigger episodes, or the interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery regimes, month-over-month increases in retail and food services sales signal reviving consumer confidence and inventory restocking, leading equity markets to reprice earnings upward and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of months with rising RSAFS during post-shock recovery periods, or the mean 5-day return in those periods is not statistically greater than zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal contracting manufacturing capacity utilization and weakening corporate pricing power, prompting equity derating and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns follow fewer than 50% of two-consecutive-month INDPRO decline episodes, or the rank correlation between INDPRO momentum and 60-day returns is not negative (p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding household income and consumption demand, which lifts expected corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following months with above-median PAYEMS growth do not outperform below-median months (Mann-Whitney U test p > 0.05), or the strategy information coefficient falls below 0.02 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.2 million units indicate a sudden freeze in residential investment and construction employment, transmitting demand weakness to durable goods and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts negative 5-day returns in fewer than 55% of episodes where HOUST is below 1200, or the mean 5-day return in triggered instances is not negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.5 percent while payroll employment is simultaneously contracting, the dual labor market deterioration signals a demand-feedback loop that forces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of joint-trigger episodes, or the interaction term between UNRATE and PAYEMS does not significantly improve explanatory power over additive models (F-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.
